event: Diagnosed with ulcerative colitis (UC) 2 years prior
Escalation of therapy from aminosalicylates to immunomodulators
Completed a three-dose induction course of infliximab 1 month prior to admission
Latest UC flare approximately 3 weeks before presentation
CT showed multiple multi-loculated abscesses in the right hepatic lobe
Largest abscess in segment 7 measured approximately 10 cm
Presence of gas in hepatic abscesses
CT showed extensive intrahepatic thrombosis of the right portal vein
CT showed diffuse colonic wall thickening
No free intraperitoneal air on CT
Appendix commented on as being normal
Abscess initially drained by interventional radiology
Cultures positive for skin flora (Gram-positive cocci in clusters and pairs)
Repeat wound cultures showed the same organisms
Antimicrobial therapy changed from vancomycin and meropenem
Antimicrobial therapy changed to ceftriaxone and metronidazole
Repeat CT 10 days after initial study showed continued improvement
Repeat CT 20 days after initial study showed continued improvement
Completed a 2-week course of oral amoxicillin-clavulanate
Discharged on an amino acid-based formula via nasogastric tube
Liver loculations improved and resolved on ultrasonography by 6 months from discharge
Normalization of liver enzymes by 6 months from discharge
Normalization of inflammatory markers by 6 months from discharge
On no UC therapy other than exclusive enteral nutrition
Remained without signs or symptoms of UC until approximately 11 months from admission
